popularStory.headline: More danico… explained
popularStory.description:
More danico

The phrase more danico[1] is a Medieval Latin legal expression which may be translated as "according to Danish custom", i.e. under Medieval Scandinavian customary law.

It designates a ty …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Gunnor de Crépon- about 0950
Information from additional sources pertaining to Gunnor and her family, and surrounding events have been included in this life sketch.
Main story and events excerpts from referenced materials.
*The Crépon family is a Norman family of Scandinavian origin living in Sauqueville, near Arques-la-Bataille, in Upper Normandy.
The Norman family of Crépon, originally from Sauqueville-Arques-la-Bataille (Upper-Normandy), owes its name to the village of Crépon (Bessin). It comes from different Scandinavian lineages belonging to the Danish, Norwegian and Swedish nobility. 

A certain William FitzOsbern Crepon is the 5th of only 20 verifiable men to have fought alongside William The Conqueror in 1066 AD, and the family played a certain very important role among the Normans throughout their reign. Today there are only approximately 93 persons with the Crepon surname in this world.
The names of Gunnor's parents are unknown, but Robert of Torigni wrote that her father was a forester from the Pays de Caux and according to Dudo of Saint-Quentin she was of noble Danish origin. Gunnor was probably born c. 950. Her family held sway in western Normandy and Gunnor herself was said to be very wealthy. Her marriage to Richard I was of great political importance, both to her husband and her progeny. Her brother was progenitor of a great Norman family. Her sisters and nieces married some of the most important nobles in Normandy.
"Gunnor was a Comtesse de Rouen by marriage to Richard I of Normandy."

• Richard II "the Good", Duke of Normandy
• Robert, Archbishop of Rouen, Count of Evreux
• Mauger, Count of Corbeil
• Robert Danus, died between 985 and 989
• another son
• Emma of Normandy, wife of two kings of England
• Maud of Normandy, wife of Odo II of Blois, Count of Blois, Champagne and Chartres
• Hawise of Normandy m. Geoffrey I, Duke of Brittany

Dudo of Saint-Quentin
The Historia, which is written alternately in prose and in verse of several metres, is divided into four parts, and deals with the history of the Normans from 852 to the death of Duke Richard in 996. It glorifies the Normans, and was largely used by William of Jumièges, Wace, Robert of Torigni, William of Poitiers and Hugh of Fleury in compiling their chronicles.

Pays de Caux
Cauchois is a notable dialect of the Norman language. The Pays de Caux is one of the remaining strongholds of the Norman language outside the Cotentin.
The principal settlements are Le Havre, Dieppe, Fécamp, Yvetot and Étretat.

References:
Medieval Lands -Charles Cawley, 2006-20.
fmg.ac. (n.d.). NORMANDY - AUMÂLE, ROUEN, EU. http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/normacre.htm#_Toc66516056.

• Wikipedia. (2021). Gunnor. 
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gunnor

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dudo_of_Saint-Quentin

https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Famille_de_Cr%C3%A9pon
• Gyaanipedia. (n.d.). Crepon (surname) - Gyaanipedia. https://en.gyaanipedia.com/wiki/Crepon_(surname) 
• Creative Commons — Attribution - Partage dans les
Mêmes Conditions 3.0 non transposé — CC BY-SA 3.0
https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/deed.fr 

Additional Reading
1) David Crouch, The Normans; the History of a Dynasty (London, New York: Hambledon Continuum, 2007), pp. 26–27
2) Elisabeth Van Houts, The Normans in Europe (Manchester: Manchester University; 12 Press, 2008), p. 58
3) Richard's marriage to Gunnor seems to have been a deliberate political move to consolidate his position by allying himself with a powerful rival family in the Cotentin.  See: D. Crouch, The Normans (2007), pp. 26 & 42;A companion to the Anglo-Norman world, eds. C. Harper-Bill; E. van Houts (2007), p. 27.

zh.lifeSummary: Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen是Unknown Parentage和Unknown Parentage的女兒，於大約0940年出生在法國諾門迪。她於0972年12月26日與Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利費艾坎普結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於從1031年1月4日到1031年1月8日在法國諾門迪費艾坎普，埋葬在法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利費艾坎普。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Crépon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English or Scottish: unexplained. Compare   Creason  .

variants.name: Creason
meanings: English or Scottish: unexplained. Compare   Creson  .
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Dutch Cruijsen, or one of its variants such as Kruijssen or Creusen, habitational names based on Dutch kruis ‘cross’ (compare Crusan and Kruis ).

nameMeanings.name: Gunnor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old Norse personal name from gunnr ‘strife’ + vor ‘cautious’.

variants.name: Gunvor
meanings: From an Old Norse personal name from gunnr ‘strife’ + vor ‘cautious’.

variants.name: Gunver
meanings: From an Old Norse personal name from gunnr ‘strife’ + vor ‘cautious’.

en.lifeSummary: Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen was born about 0940, in Normandy, France as the daughter of Unknown Parentage and Unknown Parentage. She married Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen on 26 December 0972, in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died from 4 January 1031 to 8 January 1031, in Fécamp, Normandy, France, and was buried in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Upper Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen est née environ 0940, à Normandie, France, fille de Unknown Parentage et Unknown Parentage. Elle a épousé Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen le 26 décembre 0972, à Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée du 4 janvier 1031 au 8 janvier 1031, à Fécamp, Normandie, France, et a été enterrée à Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen wurde um 0940 in Normandie, Frankreich, als Tochter von Unknown Parentage und Unknown Parentage geboren. Sie hat am 26. Dezember 0972 in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb von 4. Januar 1031 bis 8. Januar 1031 in Fécamp, Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen è nata il circa 0940 nel Normandie, Francia come figlia di Unknown Parentage e Unknown Parentage. Lei sposato Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen il 26 dicembre 0972, nel Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì da 4 gennaio 1031 a 8 gennaio 1031, nel Fécamp, Normandie, Francia, e fu sepolta a Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouenは約0940年にフランスノーマンディでUnknown ParentageとUnknown Parentageの娘として生まれた。0972年12月26日にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメフeカンップでRichard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女はから1031年1月4日まで1031年1月8日にフランスノーマンディフeカンップで死去し，フランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメフeカンップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen(은)는 대략 0940년 프랑스 노먼디에서 Unknown Parentage(와)과 Unknown Parentage의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 0972년 12월 26일 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 프e캠프에서 Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1031년 1월 4일부터 1031년 1월 8일까지 프랑스 노먼디 프e캠프에서 사망했으며 프랑스 하테-노먼디 세이인-마이팀 프e캠프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen nasceu aproximadamente 0940, em Normandie, França filha de Unknown Parentage e Unknown Parentage. Ela casou-se com Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen em 26 de dezembro de 0972, em Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu de 4 de janeiro de 1031 a 8 de janeiro de 1031, em Fécamp, Normandie, França, e foi sepultada em Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen родилась около 0940 г. в местности норманди, Франция. Её родителей звали Unknown Parentage и Unknown Parentage. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen 26 декабря 0972 г. в местности фeкамп, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла с 4 января 1031 по 8 января 1031 в местности фeкамп, норманди, Франция и была похоронена в местности фeкамп, сине-маритим, хауте-норманди, Франция.
